notes: Security review passes. Threat model exercised: (1) rate-limit bypass by presenting different token positions -- refused by check_request_token constant-time compare (F006), then rate limiter buckets on fingerprint regardless of presentation channel; (2) session replay -- expired session refused with 401 + rotate hint, expired session cannot be revived without rotation (which requires a currently-valid token in the same request); (3) plaintext leakage on rotation -- test_rotate_logs_only_fingerprint pins that the rotated string never appears in a log record. Rate-limit bucket state is per-process; a restart resets it (single-user install per D052 -- acceptable). Rate limiter cannot cause a denial of the rotate endpoint (rotate is exempt from the session-expiry rejection).
review_criteria: QA re-runs the 29 F009 tests + the full suite
